title: सुन्दरकाण्ड हनुमान स्तुति
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सुन्दरकाण्ड हनुमान स्तुति ॥

॥ श्रीरामचरितमानस सुन्दरकाण्ड से ॥

जामवन्त के बचन सुहाए। सुनि हनुमन्त हृदय अति भाए॥
तब लगि मोहि परिखेहु तुम्ह भाई। सहि दुख कन्द मूल फल खाई॥

जब लगि आवौं सीतहि देखी। होइहि काजु मोहि मन बिस्वासा॥
यह कहि नाइ सबन्हि कहुँ माथा। चलेउ हरषि हियँ धरि रघुनाथा॥

सिंधु तीर एक भूधर सुंदर। कौतुक कूदि चढ़ेउ ता ऊपर॥
बार बार रघुबीर सँभारी। तरकेउ पवनतनय बल भारी॥

जेहिं गिरि चरन देइ हनुमंता। चलेउ सो गा पाताल तुरंता॥
जिमि अमोघ रघुपति कर बाना। एही भाँति चलेउ हनुमाना॥

जलनिधि रघुपति दूत बिचारी। तैं मैनाक होहि श्रम हारी॥
हनुमान तेहि परसा कर। नाइ सीस पुनि कीन्ह प्रयान॥

राम काजु सबु करिहौं। आइ बचन सुनाइहौं तोही॥
मन महुँ करि प्रभु के बल रासा। धाइहि चलेउ रघुपति के दासा॥

लंका निसिचर निकर निवासा। रामदूत मन कीन्ह न त्रासा॥
कनक कोट बिचित्र मनि कृता। चहु दिसि कनक कोट अति भा॥

गिरि पर चढ़ि लंका तेहिं देखी। कहि न जाइ अति दुर्ग बिसेषी॥
अतिबल दानव दैत्य अपारा। लंका पुरी जहँ रक्षस राजा॥

मसक समान रूप कपि धरी। लंकहि चलेउ सुमिरि नरहरी॥
नाम लंकिनी एक निसाचरी। सो कह चलेसि मोहि निंदरी॥

जानेहि नहीं मरमु सठ मोरा। मोर अहार जहाँ लगि चोरा॥
मुठिका एक महा कपि हनी। रुधिर बमत धरनीं ढनमनी॥

पुनि सँभारि उठि सो लंका। जोरि पानि कर बिनय संसंका॥
जब रावनहि ब्रह्म बर दीन्हा। चलत बिरंचि कहा मोहि चीन्हा॥

बिकल होसि तब जानेसु।
तात मोर अति पुन्य बहूता। देखेउँ नयन राम कर दूता॥

तात स्वर्ग अपबर्ग सुख धरिअ तुलसी तरु तीर।
परम प्रकाश रूप तुम्ह देखे रामरंग बीर॥

॥ इति सुन्दरकाण्ड हनुमान स्तुति सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti ||

|| Shri Ramcharitmanas Sundarkand Se ||

Jamavant ke bachan suhaye. Suni Hanumant hriday ati bhaye.
Tab lagi mohi parikhehu tumh bhai. Sahi dukh kand mul phal khai.

Jab lagi aawaun Sitahi dekhi. Hoihi kaju mohi man bisvaasa.
Yah kahi nai saban-hi kahun matha. Chaleu harashi hriyan dhari Raghunatha.

Sindhu tir ek bhudhar sundar. Kautuk kudi chadh-eu ta upar.
Bar bar Raghubir sambhari. Tarkeu Pavan-tanay bal bhari.

Jehi giri charan dei Hanumanta. Chaleu so ga patal turanta.
Jimi amogh Raghupati kar bana. Ehi bhanti chaleu Hanumana.

Jalanidhi Raghupati dut bichari. Tain Mainak hohi shram hari.
Hanuman tehi parasa kar. Nai sis puni kinha prayan.

Ram kaju sabu karihaaun. Aai bachan sunaihaaun tohi.
Man mahun kari Prabhu ke bal rasa. Dhaihi chaleu Raghupati ke dasa.

Lanka nisichar nikar nivasa. Ram-dut man kinha na trasa.
Kanak kot bichitra mani krita. Chahu disi kanak kot ati bha.

Giri par chadhi Lanka tehi dekhi. Kahi na jai ati durg biseshi.
Atibal danav daitya apara. Lanka puri jahan rakshasa raja.

Masak saman rup Kapi dhari. Lankahi chaleu sumiri Narhari.
Nam Lankini ek nisachari. So kah chalesi mohi nindari.

Janehi nahin maramu sath mora. Mor ahar jahan lagi chora.
Muthika ek Maha-kapi hani. Rudhir bamat dharanin dhanmani.

Puni sambhari uthi so Lanka. Jori pani kar binay sasanka.
Jab Ravanahi Brahm bar dinha. Chalat Biranchi kaha mohi chinha.

Bikal hosi tab janesu.
Tat mor ati punya bahuta. Dekheun nayan Ram kar duta.

Tat swarg apabarg sukh dharia tulasi taru tir.
Param prakash rup tumh dekhe Ramrang bir.

|| Iti Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जामवन्त के सुन्दर वचन सुनकर हनुमान जी के हृदय में अत्यन्त आनन्द हुआ। उन्होंने कहा — तब तक मेरी प्रतीक्षा करना, कन्द-मूल-फल खाकर कष्ट सहना।

जब तक मैं सीताजी को देखकर आऊँ, कार्य अवश्य होगा, मेरे मन में विश्वास है। यह कहकर सबको मस्तक नवाकर, हृदय में रघुनाथ को धारण कर हर्षित होकर चले।

समुद्र तट पर एक सुन्दर पर्वत पर कौतुक से कूदकर चढ़े। बार-बार रघुवीर का स्मरण कर पवनपुत्र बड़े बल से उछले। जिस पर्वत पर हनुमान जी ने पैर रखा, वह तुरन्त पाताल में चला गया। जैसे रघुपति का अमोघ बाण, उसी भाँति हनुमान चले।

समुद्र ने रघुपति के दूत को पहचानकर मैनाक से कहा — श्रम हरण करो। हनुमान ने उसे हाथ से स्पर्श किया, मस्तक नवाया और पुनः प्रयाण किया।

लंका में राक्षसों का समूह निवास करता था, पर राम के दूत के मन में तनिक भी भय नहीं हुआ। मच्छर के समान सूक्ष्म रूप धारण कर नरहरि का स्मरण करते हुए लंका में प्रवेश किया।

लंकिनी ने कहा — मेरा भेद नहीं जानता मूर्ख! महाकपि ने एक मुट्ठी मारी। रक्त वमन करती हुई वह गिरी। फिर सँभलकर उठी और हाथ जोड़कर विनय करने लगी — मेरा बड़ा पुण्य है कि राम के दूत को नेत्रों से देखा।
benefits: Invokes the courage and strength of hanuman
Classical tradition invokes this hymn for removal of fear (bhaya-nashana) and obstacles (vighna-hara)
Invokes the blessing of the blessings of both lord rama and hanuman
Traditionally recited for removal of negative energies and evil influences
Traditionally associated with success in seemingly impossible tasks
description: The Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti, known in Sanskrit as सुन्दरकाण्ड हनुमान स्तुति, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Hanuman. Drawn from Ramcharitmanas - Sundarkand by Goswami Tulsidas, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to remove all fears and obstacles from life, bring success in seemingly impossible tasks, and destroy negative energies and evil influences. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Hanuman Jayanti and Ram Navami for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 16 verses and takes around 12 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti from Ramcharitmanas in Hindi with transliteration. Key verses of Hanuman's journey to Lanka.
relatedSearches: Sundar Kand full path online Hindi
Hanuman Lanka journey story Awadhi verses
Sundarkand for removal of obstacles troubles
Sundar Kand audio MP3 Mukesh Ravindra Jain

seoFaqs.question: What is the Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti and how does it relate to the full Sundarkand?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sundarkand Hanuman Stuti is a curated set of key chaupais and dohas drawn from the fifth book (Sundarkand) of Goswami Tulsidas's Ramcharitmanas, focusing specifically on Hanuman's heroic journey to Lanka in search of Sita. The full Sundarkand contains 60 stanzas covering Hanuman's leap across the ocean, his encounters with Mainak, Surasa and Simhika, his fight with Lankini, his meeting with Sita in Ashoka Vatika, his burning of Lanka, and his return to Sri Rama. The Stuti format extracts the most spiritually charged verses for shorter daily recitation when the full one-hour Sundarkand path is not possible.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Sundarkand and when?
seoFaqs.answer: The Sundarkand is the fifth of the seven kands of Goswami Tulsidas's Sri Ramcharitmanas, completed in Awadhi between 1574 and 1576 CE in Varanasi. Tulsidas (c.1497 to 1623 CE) composed it after his deep darshan of Hanuman, who is traditionally believed to have appeared to him and granted the vision of Sri Rama at Chitrakoot. Of all seven kands of the Manas, Sundarkand alone is held to be self-sufficient for sankalpa-purposes; reciting only Sundarkand grants the merit of the entire Ramcharitmanas, which is why it is the most independently recited section.

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Sundarkand named Sundar (beautiful)?
seoFaqs.answer: Three traditional explanations are offered. First, this kand alone is dominated by the auspicious actions of Hanuman, who is sundara in deed (sundara-karta), and Tulsidas opens it with Hanuman's seva-bhava. Second, Lanka itself stands on Mount Trikuta, one of whose three peaks is named Sundara, where Hanuman lands. Third, Bhagavan Rama himself in the Adhyatma Ramayana praises this kand as sundara because every event in it produces shubha (auspiciousness) for the listener: every obstacle Hanuman faces is overcome through Rama-smarana, modelling for the devotee how to handle worldly difficulties.

seoFaqs.question: What is the recommended way to perform Sundarkand path at home?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional path-vidhi prescribes a clean Tuesday or Saturday morning after bathing, facing east or north, before an image of Hanuman with a lit ghee or sesame oil diya, sindoor, and offerings of jaggery, gram and bananas. The complete Sundarkand takes 90 to 120 minutes; the Stuti version takes 12 to 15 minutes. Many devotees undertake an 11-week, 21-week or 41-week consecutive Tuesday sankalpa for specific concerns: removal of obstacles, freedom from court cases, marriage of a daughter, business success or recovery from illness. Group recitation (samuhik path) on Tuesday evenings in homes or Hanuman temples is widespread across North India.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Sundarkand recitation grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The Manas itself states in its concluding stuti that listening to or reciting any kand bestows specific phala, with Sundarkand being prescribed for removal of obstacles, success in seemingly impossible tasks, freedom from fear of enemies and ghosts, removal of negative energies and evil eye, recovery from chronic illness, success in court cases and difficult negotiations, and the simultaneous grace of both Hanuman and Sri Rama. The Lankini-episode within it (where Hanuman with a single fist defeats the rakshasi guarding Lanka) is the textual basis for invoking Sundarkand as a parihara against external enemies and obstructive forces.

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Mainak parvat episode in Sundarkand?
seoFaqs.answer: When Hanuman is mid-flight over the ocean, Mount Mainak (whose wings were once cut by Indra and who hides under the sea) rises up and offers Hanuman a resting place out of gratitude to Hanuman's father Vayu, who had once protected Mainak. Hanuman, recognising the courtesy, touches Mainak with his hand as acknowledgment but refuses to rest, saying Rama's work cannot be paused. This episode teaches the principle of single-pointed dedication to dharma even when comforts are offered, and the spiritual courtesy of acknowledging help received without abandoning one's mission. It is one of the most cited passages in Hanuman-bhakti discourses on focused effort.
